title: User profile page displaying another user's personal data
description: I logged into my account to update my profile information, but when I navigated to my profile page, I can see another user's contact information and order history instead of my own. The name shown is 'Sarah Johnson' but my name is 'Michael Chen'. I can see her email address (sarah.j@email.com), phone number, shipping addresses, and complete purchase history including order numbers and amounts. This is extremely concerning from a privacy perspective. I tried logging out and back in, but the same issue persists. I can still access other parts of the application normally, but the profile page consistently shows someone else's data.
logs_excerpt: INFO 2026-04-02 10:15:33 auth.middleware - User authenticated: user_id=12847, session_id=sess_xK9mP2vL8nQ4R. WARN 2026-04-02 10:15:34 profile.service - Returning profile for user_id=15923 despite authentication for user_id=12847. Potential session/cache key collision.

title: Password reset link remains valid after being used once
description: I requested a password reset for my account and received the reset link via email. I clicked the link, successfully reset my password, and logged in with the new password. However, out of curiosity, I clicked the same reset link again from my email, and it still worked - I was able to reset my password a second time using the exact same link. I used the same reset link twice and both times worked without any error or warning that the link had already been used. This seems like a security issue because if someone intercepts the reset email, they could use the link even after the legitimate user has already reset their password.

title: Internal admin dashboard accessible without authentication
description: While testing our application's public pages, I accidentally navigated to /admin in the URL bar and discovered that the internal admin dashboard is accessible without requiring any authentication. I was not logged in at all, yet I had full access to the admin panel showing user management, system settings, database statistics, and administrative controls. I could view all registered users, their email addresses, account status, and even had buttons to modify user permissions and delete accounts. This is a critical security vulnerability as anyone who discovers this URL path could gain unauthorized administrative access to the system.

CRITICAL 2026-04-04 09:45:12 security.audit - Unauthenticated access to admin dashboard detected.

title: Search results include records from other organization accounts
description: Our multi-tenant application is showing search results that include records from other organizations. When users in Organization A search for customer records, they're seeing results that include customers from Organization B and Organization C mixed in with their own data. The search functionality was working correctly until the database query optimization in v4.9.0 last week. Users can see customer names, email addresses, and order summaries from other organizations in the search results. However, when they click on these records, they get an 'Access Denied' error, so they cannot actually view the full details. This appears to be a data leak issue in the search query logic.
logs_excerpt: INFO 2026-04-05 11:30:22 search.service - Search query executed: org_id=1001, query='john', user_id=12847. DEBUG 2026-04-05 11:30:22 database.query - SQL: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE name LIKE '%john%' AND status='active' LIMIT 50. ERROR 2026-04-05 11:30:25 records.controller - Access denied: user_id=12847 (org_id=1001) attempted to access record_id=88234 (org_id=1002).

title: Mass service failures across frontend, payments, and notifications after deploy
description: Immediately after deploying v5.0.0 to production, we're experiencing widespread failures across multiple services. The frontend is showing 500 Internal Server Error on most pages, payment processing is completely down with gateway connection errors, and notification emails are failing to send. The error logs show failures in frontend rendering, payment API calls, notification service connections, and even some database queries timing out. This appears to be affecting all users and all functionality. The deployment included updates to the frontend framework, payment gateway integration, and notification service configuration. We need to identify the root cause quickly as this is a complete service outage.
